title: citrus × aurantium hystrix
intro: Citrus × aurantium hystrix, also known as the Sudachi, is a species of citrus fruit native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of limonene, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Sudachi is believed to offer various health benefits, including aiding digestion and reducing stress, due to its high vitamin C content and other bioactive compounds. Sudachi is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, to capitalize on its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
